attack_chains.name: svchost_update.exe full lifecycle
attack_chains.summary: Drive-by download, file-write, Run-key + service persistence, execution, C2 callback.

attack_chains.name: Credential theft and lateral movement
attack_chains.summary: Mimikatz credential harvest, NTLM auth to DC with stolen creds, PsExec lateral movement, MFT timestomping.

findings.description: Browser history records download of svchost_update.exe from evil-update.example.com
findings.notes: Initial infection vector. Browser cache / history under user profile.

findings.description: Suspicious executable C:\Windows\Temp\svchost_update.exe written to disk
findings.notes: Dropped payload. Look in MFT, $LogFile, USN journal.
Primary persistence mechanism. RegRipper / hivexsh on SOFTWARE hive.

findings.description: Windows Event Log 7045: Service WindowsUpdateSvc installed pointing to svchost_update.exe
Prefetch confirms execution of SVCHOST_UPDATE.EXE at 02:15 UTC
findings.notes: PECmd / prefetch parsing. Proves execution actually happened.

findings.description: Outbound C2 connection to 185.143.223.47:443 from svchost_update.exe
findings.notes: Sysmon network connect event or netstat artifact.

findings.description: PowerShell Event 4104 script block records Invoke-Mimikatz execution
NTLM authentication to domain controller 10.0.0.1 with harvested credentials (Event 4624 Type 3)
findings.notes: Security.evtx event 4624 Logon Type 3. Cross-reference with B-007 timing.

findings.description: Amcache shows PsExec.exe executed for lateral movement
MFT $SI timestamp for svchost_update.exe backdated to 2017-01-01 (timestomping)
findings.notes: Compare $SI vs $FN timestamps in MFT entry. Anti-forensics technique.
